mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and controlled inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, driving short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend levels confirms sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP revisions consistently fail to predict quarterly equity returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.01 across all rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 265.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding the threshold, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening residential investment and broader credit tightening, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline fails to predict negative equity returns when commercial real estate demand remains strong, or the signal loses power during periods of government housing subsidies.
